default_value: By default, your Windows WorkSpaces are configured to receive updates from Windows Update. To configure your own automatic update mechanisms for Windows, see the documentation for Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) and Configuration Manager. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/workspace-maintenance.html

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure your WorkSpaces image has the appropriate CIS Operating System Benchmark applied
description: Utilize the CIS Benchmark to secure the Operating system image that you are utilizing for your WorkSpaces.
rationale: Securing the Operating system with a CIS Benchmark ensures all systems remain in a secure, compliant and hardened state.
audit: Spin up a WorkSpaces instance and run a manual assessment by confirming the CIS Operating System Benchmark recommendations for the applicable operating system are applied. You can also utilize a 3rd Party Assessment tool that has been certified for the specific CIS Operating System Benchmark to automate this process.
remediation: Perform the steps below using the downloaded free version of the applicable CIS Operating System Benchmark and manually apply the recommendations for the WorkSpaces instance. Or Utilize a 3rd Party tool to assess and apply the CIS Operating System Benchmark. 1. Launch a WorkSpaces Bundle 2. Access that WorkSpace as an Administrator utilize SSH or RDP. 3. Apply the Benchmark: - Manually - Using Active Directory by creating a GPO that matches the Benchmark. - Or using a Third Party tool that will apply the Benchmark recommendations. 4. Assess the WorkSpaces instance manually or with a 3rd Party tool. 5. Create a workspace bundle that can then be used to launch your production WorkSpaces instances.
justification: Verifying that a WorkSpaces image has the appropriate CIS Operating System Benchmark applied requires inspection of the internal OS configuration within the image. AWS APIs, including boto3, AWS CLI, and AWS Config, do not expose the necessary OS-level configuration details for WorkSpaces images. While AWS Systems Manager could potentially be used to run compliance scripts on active WorkSpaces, it cannot directly validate the baseline of a custom image or bundle itself without manual intervention. Therefore, this control cannot be fully automated using AWS-native API capabilities.
manual_summary: 1. Launch a WorkSpace using the custom image or bundle under review. 2. Connect to the WorkSpace instance via RDP (for Windows) or the appropriate method for the OS. 3. Use a CIS Benchmark assessment tool (such as CIS-CAT or a relevant script) to scan the WorkSpace for compliance with the applicable CIS Operating System Benchmark. 4. Review the assessment report to confirm that all required CIS controls are applied and compliant. 5. Document the results and retain evidence (e.g., screenshots, reports) for audit purposes.
default_value: By default, this feature is not native to AWS and therefore is not enabled by default. Internal Only - General

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Restrict WorkSpaces Bundle options to organization approved versions
remediation: Preform the following to limit the bundle type Create the required AWS support case: From the Console: 1. Login in to AWS Support Center dashboard at https://console.aws.amazon.com/support/. 2. Click Create a case. 3. For Case details: - Type, choose `Account` - Category, choose `Other Account Issues` - Subject, "Limit AWS WorkSpaces instances launch to approved bundle types". - Description textbox, explain that security and compliance requires the need to limit the provisioning of WorkSpaces instances to an approved bundle type. - Contact options, leave as default or change as needed.  4. Click Submit Default Value: By default, there is no bundle restriction. This is a manual decision that must be made by technology stakeholders in your organization. References: 1. https://aws.amazon.com/workspaces/faqs/ 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/latest/adminguide/amazon- workspaces-bundles.html 3. https://aws.amazon.com/workspaces/features/ 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_manage_polici es_scps.html CIS Controls:
justification: While AWS Config or custom scripts could potentially monitor existing WorkSpaces for compliance with approved bundle types, there is no AWS API, CLI, or direct automation method to restrict or enforce which WorkSpaces bundles are available for provisioning. The actual enforcement of bundle restrictions requires submitting a support case to AWS, as described in the remediation steps, which is inherently a manual process. Automated monitoring can only alert, not enforce, and does not fulfill the intent of this control.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Support Center at https://console.aws.amazon.com/support/. 2. Click "Create a case." 3. For Case details: - Type: Select "Account." - Category: Select "Other Account Issues." - Subject: Enter "Limit AWS WorkSpaces instances launch to approved bundle types." - In the Description textbox, clearly explain that security and compliance require limiting the provisioning of WorkSpaces to only organization-approved bundle types. List the approved bundle types. 4. Submit the case and retain the case ID for audit purposes. 5. After AWS implements the restriction, attempt to launch a WorkSpace with a non-approved bundle to confirm the restriction is enforced. 6. Document the outcome and retain evidence of the restriction for compliance verification.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Workspaces images are not older than 90 days.
description: WorkSpaces images should not have a creation time stamp over 90 days.
rationale: WorkSpaces images require Operating system patches to be applied and updated and by confirming the creation date is not over 90 days old can help ensure that updates are being applied.
audit: Perform the following to determine the age of WorkSpaces images. From the Console: 1. Login to the WorkSpaces dashboard at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane click Images. 3. Review the Created date and confirm that all images are newer than 90 days. If any images are older than 90 days refer to the remediation procedure below.
remediation: To create a custom image From the Console: Note - If you are still connected to the WorkSpace, disconnect. 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane, choose WorkSpaces. 3. Select the WorkSpace and choose Actions, Create Image. - A message displays, prompting you to restart your WorkSpace before continuing. Restarting your WorkSpace updates your Amazon WorkSpaces software to the latest version. Once you have restarted your WorkSpace, repeat Step 4 of this procedure. 5. Click Next. Internal Only - General 6. Enter an image name and a description. 7. Click Create Image. While the image is being created, the status of the WorkSpace is Suspended and the WorkSpace is unavailable. In the left pane, click Images. The image is complete when the status of the WorkSpace changes to Available.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that AWS exposes WorkSpaces image metadata, including creation dates, via the `describe_workspace_images` API. This allows for a fully automated compliance check by programmatically comparing each image's creation date to the current date to ensure none are older than 90 days. No manual inspection or console-only properties are required, and the process is entirely scriptable using AWS SDKs or CLI.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: Amazon WorkSpaces Images - API/CLI Method: boto3 client method `describe_workspace_images()` or AWS CLI `aws workspaces describe-workspace-images` - Properties to Check: Each image's `Created` (creation date) field in the response - Pass Criteria: All WorkSpaces images have a `Created` date within the last 90 days from the current date - Fail Criteria: Any WorkSpaces image has a `Created` date older than 90 days from the current date
default_value: By default, images can exist for indefinite time.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure WorkSpaces that are not being utilized are removed.
description: Identify and remove any WorkSpace instances available within your AWS account that are not being utilized.
rationale: An AWS WorkSpaces instance is considered unused if has 0 (zero) known user connections registered within the past 30 days.
audit: Perform the following to ensure WorkSpaces not being utilized have been removed. From the Console: 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces dashboard at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/. 2. In the left panel click WorkSpaces 3. Choose the WorkSpaces instance that you want to examine. 4. Click on WorkSpace id link. -Verify the User Last Active attribute value is less than 30 days old 5. Repeat step 4 to verify the last user login, returned by the User Last Active attribute value, for all WorkSpaces. 6. Change the AWS region and repeat the audit process for other regions. If the User Last Active was registered more than 30 days ago (e.g. Feb 16, 2017 10:32:54 UTC), the selected WorkSpaces instance is not in use anymore and can be safely removed from your AWS account. Refer to the remediation procedure below. From the Command line: 1. Run the describe-workspaces command to list the IDs of all WorkSpaces instances available within the selected region: aws workspaces describe-workspaces --region us-east-1 --output table --query 'Workspaces[*].WorkspaceId' 2. The command should return a table with the requested WorkSpaces IDs: Internal Only - General -------------------- |DescribeWorkspaces| +------------------+ | ws-7cgsl2k65 | | ws-8d6il5kr3 | | ws-2dtyl1g47 | +------------------+ 3. Run the describe-workspaces-connection-status command using the ID of the WorkSpaces instance in the table output aws workspaces describe-workspaces-connection-status --region us-east-1 --workspace-ids ws-7cgsl2k65 --query 'WorkspacesConnectionStatus[*].LastKnownUserConnectionTimestamp' 4. The command should return the timestamp of the User last active for the selected instance: [ 1489139777.721 ] 5. Run the date command using the timestamp value returned at the previous step to convert it to a human readable date value: date -d @1489139777.721 6. Verify the User Last Active attribute value is less than 30 days old Fri Mar 10 09:56:17 UTC 2017 If the User last active date returned is more than 30 days ago, the selected WorkSpaces instance is not utilized anymore and can be safely removed from your AWS account. Refer to the remediation procedure below to remove the WorkSpaces. 7. Repeat steps 3 – 6 to verify the User Last active date for the other WorkSpaces instances listed in the current region. 8. Change the AWS region by updating the --region command parameter value and repeat steps no. 1 – 7 to perform the entire audit process for other regions.- Internal Only - General
remediation: Perform the following to remove unused WorkSpaces based on the output collected from the audit procedure From the Console: 1. Log in to WorkSpaces dashboard at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/. 2. In the left panel click WorkSpaces 3. Select the WorkSpace ID that you have identified as not being used. 4. Click Actions, Remove WorkSpaces 5. Confirm using the Audit that this is the WorkSpaces ID you should remove. 6. Click Remove WorkSpaces From the Command line: Note that running this command does not prompt you to confirm that you are removing the correct WorkSpaces ID. 1. Run terminate-workspaces command using the ID of the WorkSpaces instance from the Audit that you want to delete: aws workspaces terminate-workspaces --region us-east-1 --terminate-workspace-requests ws-0cgsl1k23
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS WorkSpaces utilization can be programmatically determined using AWS APIs. Specifically, boto3 provides methods such as `describe_workspaces()` to list WorkSpaces and `describe_workspaces_connection_status()` to check for recent user connections. No manual inspection is required, as all necessary data is exposed via the AWS SDK, making full automation feasible and reliable for this control.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: WorkSpaces (AWS End User Compute) - API/CLI Method: - boto3 `workspaces.describe_workspaces()` - boto3 `workspaces.describe_workspaces_connection_status()` - Properties to Check: - WorkSpace `State` (should be "AVAILABLE") - `ConnectionStatus` → `LastKnownUserConnectionTimestamp` - Pass Criteria: - All WorkSpaces in "AVAILABLE" state have a `LastKnownUserConnectionTimestamp` within the defined recent period (e.g., last 30 days). - Fail Criteria: - Any WorkSpace in "AVAILABLE" state has no `LastKnownUserConnectionTimestamp` within the defined recent period (e.g., last 30 days), indicating it is unused.
default_value: By default, Workspaces are not deleted after a defined period of inactivity.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure primary interface ports for Workspaces are not open to all inbound traffic.
description: Ensure that the inbound traffic of the primary network interface for all WorkSpaces is not open to all connections 0.0.0.0\00.
rationale: Attached security groups to the primary elastic network interface (ENI) to manage ports and network communication should not be open to all communication. They should be restricted to what is required by WorkSpaces, the Organization and other services.
audit: Perform the steps below to confirm security groups are configured correctly From the console: 1. Login to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Your VPCs 3. Note the VPC Id for WorkSpaces 4. In the left pane, click Security Groups 5. In the Filter security groups enter the name of the WorkSpaces VPC 6. Select the WorkSpaces Security Group 7. Click on Inbound rules 8. Confirm that there is no rule for All traffic, All, All, 0.0.0.0/0, - If there is a rule for Inbound traffic that is open to all traffic and all ip addresses refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps below to remove Inbound rule that allows all traffic from all IP addresses. From the console: 1. Login to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Your VPCs 3. Note the VPC Id for WorkSpaces 4. In the left pane, click Security Groups 5. In the Filter security groups enter the name of the WorkSpaces VPC 6. Select the WorkSpaces Security Group 7. Click on Inbound rules Internal Only - General 8. Click on Edit inbound rules 9. Click on Delete for the rule that shows -All traffic, All, All, 0.0.0.0/0, - 10. Click on Save rules Note - Make sure you have all the required ports add to Inbound rules as listed in the WorkSpaces documentation outlined in the references so that connectivity to WorkSpaces is not impacted.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the security group rules associated with AWS Workspaces can be audited programmatically using AWS APIs. The necessary information—such as Workspace associations, VPCs, and security groups—is accessible via boto3 and AWS CLI. While the relationship between Workspaces and security groups is indirect (via directories and VPCs), all required data is available through API calls, making full automation feasible and reliable.
automation_summary: - **AWS Resource/Service:** AWS Workspaces, EC2 Security Groups - **API/CLI Method:** - `boto3.client('workspaces').describe_workspaces()` - `boto3.client('workspaces').describe_workspace_directories()` - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_security_groups()` - **Properties to Check:** - For each Workspace, determine the associated directory and VPC. - Identify security groups attached to the Workspace's VPC/subnet. - Inspect each security group's inbound rules for entries allowing traffic from `0.0.0.0/0` (all IPv4) or `::/0` (all IPv6) on any port. - **Pass Criteria:** - No inbound security group rule associated with any Workspace allows unrestricted access (`0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`) to primary interface ports. - **Fail Criteria:** - Any inbound rule on a security group associated with a Workspace allows traffic from `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0` to primary interface ports.
default_value: By default, the following open inbound ports are open: Inbound TCP on port 4172. This is used for establishment of the streaming connection on the PCoIP protocol. Inbound UDP on port 4172. This is used for streaming user input on the PCoIP protocol. Inbound TCP on port 4489. This is used for access using the web client. Inbound TCP on port 8200. This is used for management and configuration of the WorkSpace. Inbound TCP on ports 8201-8250. These ports are used for establishment of the streaming connection and for streaming user input on the DCV protocol.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure FIPS Endpoint encryption is enabled for WorkSpaces.
description: To meet a high level of security and comply with different compliance standards, you must use Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) endpoint encryption at the directory level with WorkSpaces.
rationale: You must also use an AWS Region that is authorized for the same compliance standard that you are trying to achieve.
audit: Perform the steps below to determine if FIPS endpoint encryption is enabled. From the Console: 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane, click Directories. 3. Select the directory id link. 4. Scroll to the Endpoint encryption section. 5. Endpoint Encryption should read - FIPS 140-2 Validated Mode. If Endpoint Encryption is not listed as FIPS 140-2 Validated Mode refer to the remediation procedure below.
remediation: Perform the steps below to enable FIPS endpoint encryption at the directory level From the Console: 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane, click Directories. 3. Verify that the directory does not have any existing WorkSpaces associated with it. 4. Select the directory id link. 5. Click Actions, Update Details. 6. Scroll to the Endpoint encryption section and select Edit Internal Only - General 7. For Endpoint Encryption, choose FIPS 140-2 Validated Mode. 8. Click Save. You can now create WorkSpaces from this directory that utilize FIPS endpoint encryption modules.
justification: Both AI opinions agree that this control can be audited using AWS APIs, specifically via boto3 or AWS CLI. The key setting—whether a WorkSpaces directory is using a FIPS endpoint—is exposed through the `describe_workspace_directories` API, which returns the `EnableWorkDocs` and, more importantly, the `WorkspaceCreationProperties` object. Within this, the `CustomSecurityGroupId` and `EnableInternetAccess` are present, but for FIPS endpoint, the relevant property is `WorkspaceAccessProperties` with the field `DeviceTypeFIPS`. This allows for programmatic verification of FIPS endpoint usage. Therefore, automation is both feasible and reliable.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: Amazon WorkSpaces Directories - API/CLI Method: - boto3: `workspaces.describe_workspace_directories()` - AWS CLI: `aws workspaces describe-workspace-directories` - Properties to Check: - In the response, under each Directory, check `WorkspaceAccessProperties.DeviceTypeFIPS`. - The value should be `ENABLED` for FIPS endpoint encryption to be considered enabled. - Pass Criteria: - All WorkSpaces Directories have `WorkspaceAccessProperties.DeviceTypeFIPS` set to `ENABLED`. - Fail Criteria: - Any WorkSpaces Directory has `WorkspaceAccessProperties.DeviceTypeFIPS` not set to `ENABLED` (i.e., `DISABLED` or missing).
default_value: By default, FIPS is disabled. TLS 1.2 is the default encryption standard for AWS Workspaces.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure WorkSpaces API requests flow through a VPC Endpoint
description: For any WorkSpaces API requests setup the connection through an interface endpoint in your VPC.
rationale: Utilizing a VPC interface endpoint for WorkSpaces API requests keeps the communication within the AWS network.
audit: Perform the steps to determine if WorkSpaces is using a VPC endpoint for API From the Command line: 1. Run the WorkSpaces `describe-workspace-budles' command aws workspaces describe-workspace-bundles --endpoint-url VPC_Endpoint_ID.workspaces.Region.vpce.amazonaws.com 2. Example output of that command: --endpoint-name Endpoint_Name --body "Endpoint_Body" --content-type "Content_Type" Output_File Confirm the --endpoint-name is equal to the VPC Endpoint that you have created. If the endpoint name does not match what you created or is blank, refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps below if you need to create a VPC interface endpoint. From the Console 1. Log in to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Endpoints Internal Only - General 3. Click Create Endpoint. 4. For Service category, ensure that AWS services is selected. 5. For Service Name, choose Workspaces. For Type, ensure that it indicates Interface. 6. Complete the following information. - For VPC, select a VPC in which to create the endpoint. - For Subnets, select the subnets (Availability Zones) in which to create the endpoint network interfaces. Not all Availability Zones may be supported for all AWS services. - To enable private DNS for the interface endpoint, for Enable Private DNS Name, select the check box. - For Security group, select the security groups to associate with the endpoint network interfaces. 8. Click Create endpoint From the Command line 1. Run the create-vpc-endpoint command aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint --vpc-id vpc-ec43eb89 --vpc-endpoint-type Interface --service-name com.amazonaws.us-east-1.elasticloadbalancing --subnet-id subnet-abababab --security-group-id sg-1a2b3c4d In the output that's returned, take note of the DnsName fields. You can use these DNS names to access the AWS service. Next perform the steps to add the Endpoint to the WorkSpace image From the Command line 1. Run the copy-workspace-image command including the endpoint url you just created. aws workspaces copy-workspace-image --endpoint-url VPC_Endpoint_ID.workspaces.Region.vpce.amazonaws.com
impact: This feature can only be used for connecting to WorkSpaces API endpoints.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that verifying whether WorkSpaces API requests flow through a VPC Endpoint can be accomplished using AWS APIs, specifically by querying VPC endpoints via boto3 or AWS CLI. The required properties—such as the existence, state, service association, and policy of VPC endpoints—are all exposed through the AWS EC2 API. No manual inspection or console-only steps are necessary, making this control fully automatable.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: VPC Endpoints (for AWS WorkSpaces) - API/CLI Method: boto3 EC2 client’s describe_vpc_endpoints() - Properties to Check: - ServiceName: Should match `com.amazonaws.<region>.workspaces` - State: Must be `available` - VPC association: Must be associated with the correct VPC(s) - PolicyDocument: Should allow WorkSpaces API actions - Pass Criteria: - At least one VPC endpoint exists for `com.amazonaws.<region>.workspaces` - The endpoint is in `available` state - The endpoint is associated with the intended VPC(s) - The endpoint policy allows required WorkSpaces API actions - Fail Criteria: - No VPC endpoint exists for WorkSpaces in the region - Endpoint is not in `available` state - Endpoint is not associated with the correct VPC(s) - Endpoint policy does not allow necessary WorkSpaces API actions
default_value: By default, this feature is not enabled and must be configured in the VPC console.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Radius server is using the recommended security protocol
description: The authentication protocol between the Microsoft AD DCs and the RADIUS server supported are PAP, CHAP, MS-CHAPv1, and MS-CHAPv2.
rationale: MS-CHAPv2 provides the strongest security of the options supported.
audit: Perform the steps to check multi-factor authentication using the radius server protocol is set to MS-CHAP v2. From the Console: For AWS Managed AD based environments; 1. Log in to the Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Choose the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section, confirm that the Protocol is set to MS- CHAPv2. For directory connector / self-managed AD environments 1. Log in to the AWS Workspaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Select the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directories page, scroll to the Multi-factor authentication section and select Edit. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section confirm that, in the Protocol field MS- CHAPv2 is selected from the dropdown list. If it is not set to MS-CHAPv2 refer to the remediation steps below. Internal Only - General
remediation: Perform the steps below to set the protocol to MS-CHAPv2 for multi-factor authentication. From the console: For AWS Managed AD based environments; 1. Log in to the Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 4. In the Multi-factor authentication section, choose Actions, and then choose Edit. 5. On the Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) page change the following value: 6. Protocol - MS-CHAPv2 7. Click Save. For directory connector / self-managed AD environments 1. Log in to the AWS Workspaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Select the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directories page, scroll to the Multi-factor authentication section and select Edit. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section modify the protocol using the dropdown menu to be MS-CHAPv2 from the currently selected option. 6. Click Save once settings are as desired.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS does not currently expose the RADIUS server security protocol configuration (specifically MS-CHAPv2 for MFA) via any public API, AWS CLI, or SDK. This setting is only accessible and configurable through the AWS Management Console UI for Directory Service and WorkSpaces. There is no AWS Config rule or resource property that can be queried or monitored for this protocol setting, making full automation impossible at this time.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2. 2. In the left pane, select "Directories." 3. Click on the relevant Directory. On the Directory details page, select the "Networking & security" tab. 4. In the "Multi-factor authentication" section, choose "Actions," then "Edit." 5. On the "Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA)" page, verify that the "Protocol" is set to "MS-CHAPv2." 6. If not set, change the protocol to "MS-CHAPv2" and save the configuration. 7. Confirm that the protocol change has been saved and is reflected in the configuration. 8. Document the setting (screenshot or note) for audit evidence.
default_value: By default, this is dependent on your RADIUS server.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure User Access Logging is enabled
description: User Access Logging can record the following user events: • Session Start - when a WorkSpaces Web sessions begins. • Session End - when a WorkSpaces Web session ends. • URL Navigation - when a user loads a URL. User Access logging can be setup to record user events.
rationale: Logging user activity will assist in event correlation if response to an incident is needed.
audit: From the Console: 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces-web/ 2. In the left pane, click Web portals. 3. Click the link for correspoinding web portal. 4. Scroll to the User access logging section 5. Verify the Kinesis data stream arn is set. If no Kinesis data streams are listed are defined then user access logging is not enabled From the Command Line: 1. From the command line run the list-user-access-logging-settings aws workspaces-web list-user-access-logging-settings --output table 2. The command should output a table with the listed settings. If no settings are defined then user access logging is not enabled Internal Only - General ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListUserAccessLoggingSettings | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ || userAccessLoggingSettings || |+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+| || kinesisStreamArn | arn:aws:kinesis:[region]:[account]:stream/[stream] || || userAccessLoggingSettingsArn| arn:aws:workspaces-web:[region]:[account]:userAccessLoggingSettings/[stream] || |+------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+|
remediation: From the Console: 1. Log in to the Amazon Kinesis console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/kinesis/home 2. In the left pane, click Data Streams then Create data stream. 3. Enter a name for your data stream. The name must be prefixed with amazon- workspaces-web 4. Select the desired data stream capacity and click Create data stream 5. Log into the Amazon WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/v2/home 6. In the left pane click Web Portals 7. Click the link for the web portal you wish to edit. 8. Click Edit 9. Scroll to User access logging and select the Kinesis data stream you created above. 10. Click Save From the Command Line: 1. Run the create-user-access-logging-settings command aws workspaces-web create-user-access-logging-settings --kinesis-stream-arn <kinesis_data_stream_arn>. --output table 2. The output will return a list of settings Internal Only - General -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | CreateUserAccessLoggingSettings | +------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | userAccessLoggingSettingsArn| arn:aws:workspaces-web:[region]:[account]:userAccessLoggingSettings/[guid] | +------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/workspaces-web/latest/adminguide/data-protection-logging.html Internal Only - General 4 WorkDocs This section contains recommendations for configuring the WorkDocs service and any additional resources required. Internal Only -
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the status of User Access Logging for Amazon WorkSpaces Web can be programmatically verified using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The relevant settings and their association with a Kinesis Data Stream are exposed via the AWS WorkSpaces Web API, making automation feasible and reliable. There is no requirement for manual inspection, as all necessary configuration data is accessible through API calls.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: Amazon WorkSpaces Web (User Access Logging Settings), Amazon Kinesis Data Streams - API/CLI Method: - `boto3.client('workspaces-web').list_user_access_logging_settings()` - `boto3.client('workspaces-web').describe_user_access_logging_settings()` - Properties to Check: - Presence of `userAccessLoggingSettingsArn` for each WorkSpaces Web portal - Association of the logging settings with a valid Kinesis Data Stream ARN - Status of the Kinesis Data Stream (should be ACTIVE) - (Optional but recommended) Permissions on the Kinesis Data Stream to ensure WorkSpaces Web can write logs - Pass Criteria: - Each WorkSpaces Web portal has a `userAccessLoggingSettingsArn` configured - The associated Kinesis Data Stream exists and is in ACTIVE status - (Optional) The Kinesis Data Stream has the necessary permissions for logging - Fail Criteria: - No `userAccessLoggingSettingsArn` is configured for a portal - The referenced Kinesis Data Stream does not exist or is not ACTIVE - (Optional) Insufficient permissions for logging
default_value: By default, user access logging is not enabled.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Administrators of WorkDocs is defined using IAM
description: To allow users to administer Amazon WorkDocs resources, you must create an IAM policy that explicitly grants them the correct permissions. This policy should then be attached to the group or role defined for this administration.
rationale: WorkDocs Administrators control access and authorization for users of the WorkDocs resources.
audit: Perform the following to determine what policies are created: From the Console: 1. Login in and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the left pane click on Groups. 3. Click on the group name that should administer WorkDocs. 4. Click on Permissions and confirm that the AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess policy is attached. 5. Click on Users and confirm that the list of names are the users approved to administer WorkDocs. 6. To confirm that the WorkDocs policy (AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess) for admin control is attached to the correct group. From the Command Line: 1. Run the list-attached-group-policies. aws iam list-attached-group-policies --group-name <workdocs_group_name> 2. Confirm that the list of users in that Group is correct aws iam get-group --group-name <workdocs_group_name> If the AWS manage policy or a custom WorkDocs Full Access policy is not attached to the group or the users in the group list is not correct refer to the remediation below. Internal Only - General
remediation: Perform the following to create an IAM group and assign the Amazon WorkDocs Full Access policy to it: From the Console: 1. Log in to the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the left pane, click Groups and then click Create New Group. 3. In the Group Name box, type the name of the group and then click Next Step. 4. In the list of policies, select the check box for AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess 5. Click Next Step. 6. Click Create Group Perform the following to add a user to a Amazon WorkDocs Full Access group: 1. Log in to the the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the left pane, click Groups 3. Select the group you created above 4. Click Add Users To Group 5. Select the users to be added to the group 6. Click Add Users
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that all necessary IAM group and policy information is accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The control requires verifying that at least one IAM group has the AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess policy attached and that users are assigned to this group, which can be fully automated by querying IAM group memberships and attached policies. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: IAM Groups, IAM Policies, IAM Users - API/CLI Method: - `list_groups()` (boto3: iam.list_groups) - `list_attached_group_policies(GroupName=...)` (boto3: iam.list_attached_group_policies) - `get_group(GroupName=...)` (boto3: iam.get_group) - Properties to Check: - At least one IAM group with the `AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess` policy attached - At least one user assigned to that group - Pass Criteria: - There exists at least one IAM group with the `AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess` policy attached AND at least one user is a member of that group. - Fail Criteria: - No IAM group has the `AmazonWorkDocsFullAccess` policy attached, OR such a group exists but has no users assigned.
default_value: By default, IAM users and roles don't have permission to create or modify Amazon WorkDocs resources.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure MFA is enabled for WorkDoc users
description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of authentication assurance beyond traditional username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to Amazon WorkDocs, they will be prompted for their user name and password as well as for an authentication code from their MFA token.
rationale: Enabling MFA provides increased security to a user name and password as it requires the user to possess a solution that displays a time-sensitive authentication code.
audit: Perform the steps below to confirm MFA setup and configuration. From the console: 1. Log in to the Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2 2. Select Directories. 3. Choose the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section, Confirm Radius status is set to Enabled. 6. Open the WorkDocs console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 7. In the Manage Your WorkDocs Sites page, select the desired site and choose Actions and Manage MFA. 8. Confirm the values are set correctly. Multi-factor authentication is available when the RADIUS Status reads Enabled. From the Command line: Internal Only - General 1. Run describe-directories command to list the identifiers of all the Active Directory (AD) Connector directories, available in the selected AWS region: aws ds describe-directories --region us-east-1 --output table --query 'DirectoryDescriptions[*].DirectoryId' 2. The command output should return a table with the requested resource IDs: --------------------- |DescribeDirectories| +-------------------+ | d-12345abcde | | d-abcd012345 | | d-aabbcc1234 | +-------------------+ 3. Run describe-directories command using the ID of the AD Connector directory to get the status of the RADIUS MFA server connection: aws ds describe-directories --region us-east-1 --directory-ids d-12345abcde --query 'DirectoryDescriptions[*].RadiusStatus' 4. The command output should return the requested status information: [] 5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 to determine the MFA status for other AD Connector directories. 6. Change the AWS region by updating the --region command parameter value and repeat steps 1 – 5 to perform the audit process for other regions. If describe-directories command output returns an empty array, as shown in the example above, there is no RADIUS MFA server configured for the selected AD Directory, therefore the resource does not have Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) protection enabled. Refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to setup MFA on the server and in WorkDocs. From the Console: Internal Only - General 1. Identify the IP address of your RADIUS MFA server and your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 2. In the AWS Directory Service console navigation pane, select Directories. 3. Choose the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section, choose Actions, and then choose Enable. 6. On the Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) page, provide the following values: - Display label - Provide a label name. - RADIUS server DNS name or IP addresses - Port - default 1812 - Shared secret code - Confirm shared secret code To enable multi-factor authentication in WorkDocs: 1. Open WorkDocs console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 2. In the Manage Your WorkDocs Sites page, select the desired site and choose Actions and Manage MFA. 3. Enter the following values: - Enable Multi-Factor Authentication - Check to enable multi-factor authentication. - RADIUS server IP address(es) - The IP addresses of your RADIUS server endpoints - Port - The port that your RADIUS server is using for communications. Default RADIUS server port (1812) - Shared secret code - The shared secret code that was specified when your RADIUS endpoints were created. - Confirm shared secret code - Protocol - MS-CHAPv2 - Server timeout - (in seconds) - 20 - Max retries - 3 4. Choose Enable. Internal Only - General Multi-factor authentication is available when the RADIUS Status changes to Enabled. To enable RADIUS-based MFA protection for your Active Directory (AD) Connector directories, perform the following actions: Note: Enabling Multi-Factor authentication for AD Connector directories using the AWS Management Console is not currently supported. From the Command line: 1. Run the enable-radius command: aws ds enable-radius --region us-east-1 --directory-id <value> --radius-settings { "RadiusServers": ["radius.<your-radius-server>.com"],"RadiusPort": 1812,"RadiusTimeout": 20,"RadiusRetries": 3,"SharedSecret": "radiusmfa","AuthenticationProtocol": "MS-CHAPv2","DisplayLabel": "RADIUS Multi-Factor Authentication","UseSameUsername": true } 2. Repeat step 1 for other AD Connectors and the Selected regions.
impact: To enable MFA for Amazon WorkDocs you require a RADIUS server or a plugin to a RADIUS server already implemented in your environment. Multi-factor authentication is not available for Simple AD. You can enable multi-factor authentication for AD Connector if you have Active Directory running on-premises or in EC2 instances.
justification: While the first opinion suggests automation via IAM APIs, the second opinion correctly points out that Amazon WorkDocs users are not always managed as IAM users; they may be provisioned via AWS Directory Service, SAML, or other identity providers. AWS APIs (including boto3 and AWS CLI) do not provide a direct method to enumerate all WorkDocs users and check their MFA status across all possible identity sources. Therefore, a reliable, comprehensive automated check is not feasible.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console as an administrator. 2. Navigate to the Amazon WorkDocs console. 3. Select the relevant WorkDocs site. 4. Review the user directory to identify all active WorkDocs users. 5. Determine the identity provider in use (AWS Directory Service, SAML, or IAM). 6. For AWS Directory Service or SAML, access the respective identity provider’s management console (e.g., Microsoft Active Directory, Okta, ADFS). 7. Verify that MFA is enforced for all users who have access to WorkDocs, according to the identity provider’s MFA policy settings. 8. Document the MFA enforcement policy and provide evidence (e.g., screenshots or policy exports) that MFA is enabled for all WorkDocs users. 9. Confirm compliance by ensuring that all user accounts require MFA at login.
default_value: By default, MFA is not enabled in AWS Workdocs.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Workdocs access is limited to a range of allowable IP addresses
description: Access to WorkDocs can be limited to an allowed range of IP addresses.
rationale: Using IP address allow lists, you define and permit access to your WorkDocs site from trusted networks.
audit: Perform these steps to review the list of IP addresses allowed to access WorkDocs From the Console: 1. Log into the AWS console. 2. Navigate to WorkDocs or go to WorkDocs Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 3. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 4. For IP Allow List, choose Change. 5. Review the IP address ranges and any single IP addresses 6. Click Cancel. If the IP address ranges do not match trusted networks refer to the remediation below to create or edit the IP Allow list.
remediation: Perform the steps below to create or edit the IP Allow list for WorkDocs From the Console: 1. Log into the AWS console. 2. Navigate to WorkDocs or go to WorkDocs Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 3. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 4. For IP Allow List, choose Change. Internal Only - General 5. For Enter CIDR value, enter the IP address ranges to allowlist. To allow access from a single IP address, specify /32 as the CIDR prefix. 6. Click Add. 7. Click Save Changes.
impact: IP Lists currently only work for IPv4 addresses and denying access through an IP list is not supported.
justification: AWS WorkDocs does not currently expose IP restriction settings via AWS APIs, SDKs (such as boto3), AWS CLI, or AWS Config. The configuration for allowable IP addresses is managed within the WorkDocs administrative console and is not accessible for direct programmatic inspection or enforcement. Both AI opinions correctly note the lack of automation capability, with the second opinion suggesting indirect monitoring via AWS Config or CloudTrail; however, these services do not provide visibility into the specific IP restriction settings required by this control.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the WorkDocs service. 2. Select the relevant WorkDocs site and access the administrative settings. 3. Locate the section for network or access control settings (often under "Site settings" or "Access controls"). 4. Review the configuration for IP address restrictions and verify that only the approved range(s) of IP addresses are permitted. 5. Confirm that the configuration matches organizational policy and that no unauthorized IP ranges are allowed. 6. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
default_value: By default, no IP addresses are allowed.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Utilize site wide activity feed for monitoring.
justification: While the control language is somewhat generic, AWS provides robust APIs (via boto3, AWS CLI, and AWS Config) to programmatically verify the presence and configuration of site-wide activity feeds such as AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch. These services are designed to capture and monitor activity across AWS accounts, fulfilling the intent of a "site wide activity feed." The verification of their existence and correct configuration (e.g., CloudTrail enabled, logging to a secure S3 bucket, multi-region trails, etc.) can be fully automated using AWS APIs.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: AWS CloudTrail (account-level), optionally AWS CloudWatch for log delivery - API/CLI Method: - boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails() - boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status() - boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors() - Properties to Check: - At least one trail is present and enabled - Trail is logging management events for all regions (isMultiRegionTrail=True) - Logging is currently enabled (IsLogging=True) - Logs are delivered to a secure S3 bucket - Pass Criteria: - CloudTrail is enabled and logging for all regions - Management events are being captured - Logs are delivered to a secure S3 bucket - Fail Criteria: - No trails enabled, or not multi-region - Logging is disabled - Management events not captured - No S3 log delivery configured

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure new users can only be invited from allowed domains.
description: Users that are allowed access to shared files or folders in WorkDocs should be limited to specific domains.
rationale: To control who should be allowed to join your WorkDocs site, users should be limited on who they can invite sharing files or folders with new people from the specified domains.
audit: Perform the steps to confirm WorkDocs file and sharing folders is controlled by specified domains. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - Invite settings 4. Confirm that only Users can invite new people from a few specific domains by sharing files or folders with them 5. Confirm the listed Domains is accurate. If the setting is not set to "Users can invite new people from a few specific domains by sharing files or folders with them" or the domains listed is not accurate refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps to set WorkDocs file and sharing folders to be controlled by specified domains. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - Invite settings Internal Only - General 4. Select Users can invite new people from a few specific domains by sharing files or folders with them 5. Add in or edit the listed allowed Domains.
justification: While some directory and user information can be queried via AWS APIs (such as with WorkSpaces or Directory Service), the specific configuration for restricting user invitations to allowed email domains is not exposed through AWS APIs, CLI, or SDKs. This setting is typically managed within the AWS Management Console or external identity providers and is not programmatically retrievable or verifiable. Therefore, a manual review of the relevant console settings is required to ensure compliance.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console. 2. Navigate to the relevant End User Compute service (e.g., AWS WorkSpaces, AppStream 2.0, or the specific service in use). 3. Access the user management or directory settings section (often under "Directories," "User Invitations," or "Identity Providers"). 4. Locate the configuration for allowed domains or domain restrictions for user invitations. 5. Review the list of allowed domains and verify that only approved domains (as per organizational policy) are present. 6. Document the allowed domains and compare them against the organization's approved list. 7. Confirm that no unauthorized domains are permitted for user invitations.
default_value: By default, this setting is disabled.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure only specific users are allowed to invite external users
description: The organization should only allow administrators the ability to invite external users to the WorkDocs site.
rationale: If anyone can invite a user outside of the organization it could potentially lead to security or information leak.
audit: Perform the steps to confirm Only Administrators can invite new external users for WorkDocs. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - external invites 4. Confirm that only Only administrators can invite new external users If this is not set to "Only administrators can invite new external users" refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps to Set Only Administrators can invite new external users for WorkDocs. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - external invites 4. Select Only administrators can invite new external users - Only administrators can invite external users to use Amazon WorkDocs. Internal Only - General
justification: The control requires verifying that only specific users are allowed to invite external users, which is governed by IAM permissions (e.g., actions like organizations:InviteAccountToOrganization). These permissions are fully exposed via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI), allowing programmatic inspection of which users, groups, or roles have these permissions. While organizational policy decisions about "who should have access" are subjective, the technical enforcement and verification of who actually has the permission is entirely automatable.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: IAM Policies, IAM Users, IAM Roles, IAM Groups - API/CLI Method: - boto3 iam.list_policies() - boto3 iam.list_policy_versions() - boto3 iam.get_policy_version() - boto3 iam.list_entities_for_policy() - boto3 iam.get_user_policy(), get_group_policy(), get_role_policy() - Properties to Check: - Policy statements granting organizations:InviteAccountToOrganization or similar invite permissions. - Which users, groups, or roles are attached to these policies. - Pass Criteria: - Only the explicitly approved users/roles/groups (as defined by organizational policy) have the invite permission. - Fail Criteria: - Any user, group, or role outside the approved list has the invite permission.
default_value: By default, this is dependent on the policies assigned.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure publicly shareable links is not allowed in WorkDocs
description: The organization should not allow publicly shareable links for WorkDocs.
rationale: If a user can create and send a publicly shareable links allowing a file to be viewed by people outside of the organization it could potentially lead to an security or information leak.
audit: Perform the steps to confirm publicly shareable links for WorkDocs is not allowed. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - public share settings 4. Confirm that - No public sharing is selected. If this is not selected choice refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps to set set publicly shareable links for WorkDocs to not allowed. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Click Security 3. Under - public share settings 4. Select - No public sharing. - Users cannot send view links to anyone outside the organization.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS WorkDocs exposes organization configuration settings, including public link sharing permissions, via the AWS API (boto3 and AWS CLI). The relevant configuration can be programmatically checked using the `describe_organization_configuration` method, making this control fully automatable. There is no need for manual inspection as all required properties are accessible via AWS APIs.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: WorkDocs Site Configuration - API/CLI Method: boto3 workdocs.describe_organization_configuration(), or AWS CLI: aws workdocs describe-organization-configuration - Properties to Check: - Response field: `OrganizationConfiguration.EnablePublicSharing` - Pass Criteria: - `EnablePublicSharing` is set to `False` (publicly shareable links are not allowed) - Fail Criteria: - `EnablePublicSharing` is set to `True` (publicly shareable links are allowed)
default_value: By default, publicly shareable links are disabled.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure any user that has not accessed WorkDocs in 30 days is set to inactive.
description: User accounts that are not actively using the WorkDocs service should be set to inactive after a period of 30 days.
rationale: Inactive accounts may appear to not pose a problem but they can provide unauthorized access to files within WorkDocs.
audit: Perform the following steps to review list of users From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator 2. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 3. Under Manage Users, choose Download user. 4. For Download user, choose All users 5. Review the file to determine if any users have not accessed WorkDocs in the past 30 days. If you find any users that have not accessed WorkDocs in the past 30 days refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the steps below to disable a user's access by changing their status to Inactive. From the WorkDocs Admin control panel 1. Log in to WorkDocs as an Administrator. 2. Under My Account, click Open admin control panel. 3. Under Manage Users, choose the pencil icon next to the user's name that needs to be set as inactive. 4. Choose Inactive, and Click Save Changes Internal Only - General The inactivated user no longer has access to your Amazon WorkDocs site.
impact: Changing a user to Inactive status does not delete their files, folders, or feedback from your Amazon WorkDocs site.
justification: Both AI opinions agree that this control can be automated using AWS APIs. Amazon WorkDocs exposes user information and activity data via the AWS SDK (boto3), specifically through the `describe_users()` API. While the exact "last activity" timestamp may not be a direct property, it can be inferred from user metadata or, if needed, by parsing CloudTrail logs for WorkDocs activity. This process does not require manual inspection, as all necessary data is accessible programmatically.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: Amazon WorkDocs Users - API/CLI Method: - Primary: `boto3.client('workdocs').describe_users()` - Supplementary (if needed): Parse AWS CloudTrail logs for WorkDocs activity events - Properties to Check: - `LastActivityTimestamp` (if available in user metadata) - If not directly available, extract last activity from CloudTrail events related to user actions in WorkDocs - Pass Criteria: - All users have a `LastActivityTimestamp` within the past 30 days, or are already set to inactive if not. - Fail Criteria: - Any user has not accessed WorkDocs in the past 30 days and is still marked as active.
default_value: By default, there is no setting to manage inactive users.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure AppStream is utilizing its own virtual private cloud (VPC)
description: AppStream 2.0 should be configured using a VPC with Private subnets and a NAT Gateway.
rationale: For AppStream 2.0 the public subnet will have direct access to the internet through the NAT gateway. This setup allows the streaming instances in your private subnets to connect to the internet or other AWS services.
audit: Perform the following to determine if a VPC is setup for AppStream 2.0 correctly. From Console: 1. Login to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Your VPCs 3. Select the VPC for AppStream 2.0 and take note of the name and the VPC ID 4. In the left pane, click Subnets 5. Confirm you have 3 subnets labeled and associated with the VPC 1 AppStream Public Subnet and 2 AppStream Private Subnets 6. Confirm the AppStream Public Subnet is configured correctly. o Select AppStream Public subnet Description tab - VPC matches `AppStream ID and name` Route Table tab - verify contains rules `-Example` - Destination - 10.0.0.0/20, Target - local `-Example` - Destination - 0.0.0.0/0, Target - internet_gateway_ID 7. Confirm the 2 AppStream Private Subnets are configured correctly. o Select AppStream Private subnet 1 Internal Only - General Description Tab - VPC matches `AppStream ID and name` and note Availability zone Route Table tab - verify contains routes `-Example` - Destination - 10.0.0.0/20, Target - local `-Example` - Destination - 0.0.0.0/0, Target - nat_gateway_ID `-Example- optional` - Destination - S3bucket_enpoint_ID, Target - storage_vpce_ID - Select AppStream Private subnet 2 Description Tab - VPC matches `AppStream ID and name` and Availability zone is set to something different than Private subnet 1 Route Table tab - verify contains routes `-Example` - Destination - 10.0.0.0/20, Target - local `-Example` - Destination - 0.0.0.0/0, Target - nat_gateway_ID `-Example- optional` - Destination - S3bucket_enpoint_ID, Target - storage_vpce_ID If The AppStream VPC, subnets and route tables are not configured correctly refer to the remediation procedure below.
remediation: Perform the steps below to create a VPC, subnets and routing table for AppStream 2.0 From the Console Allocate an Elastic IP address 1. Login in to the Amazon VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Elastic IPs. 3. Click Allocate new address. 4. Then click on Allocate. 5. Make a note of the Elastic IP address. 6. Click Close. Create a New VPC with one public subnet and two private subnet's. 1. Login to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. Click Launch VPC Wizard. 3. Choose VPC with Public and Private Subnet'sand then clickSelect`. 4. Configure the VPC as follows: Internal Only - General - `IPv4 CIDR block` - enter a CIDR block from the private (non-publicly routable) IP address range - example. 10.0.0.0/16. - `IPv6 CIDR block` - Accept the default value - No IPv6 CIDR Block - `VPC name` enter a name for the VPC (example, AppStream VPC). - `Public subnet's IPv4 CIDR` - enter a CIDR block from the private (non-publicly routable) IP address range - ie. 10.0.0.0/24. - `Availability Zone` - Accept the default value - No Preference - `Public subnet name` - enter a name for the subnet (example, AppStream Public Subnet) - `Private subnet's IPv4 CIDR` - enter the CIDR block for the subnet. - `Availability Zone` - Accept the default value - No Preference - `Private subnet name` - enter a name for the subnet (example, AppStream Private Subnet 1). - `Elastic IP Allocation ID` - enter the Elastic IP address that you created. - `Service Endpoints` - Accept the default value - Blank - `Enable DNS hostnames` - Accept the default value - Yes - `Hardware tenancy` - Accept the default value - Default 5. Click on Create VPC ***Note that it takes several minutes to set up your VPC. After the VPC is created, choose OK. Create the Second Private subnet to the VPC 1. In the left pane, choose Subnets. 2. Click Create subnet. - `Name tag` - enter a name for the private subnet (example, AppStream Private subnet 2). - `VPC` - select the VPC that you created for AppStream 2.0. - `Availability Zone` - select a different one than you are using for AppStream2 Private subnet 1. - `IPv4 CIDR block` - enter the CIDR block for the subnet. 3. Click Create. Verify and Name the Route Tables 1. In the left pane, choose Subnets 2. Select the public subnet that you created (example, AppStream Public subnet) 3. On the Route Table tab, click the ID of the route table (example, rtb-12345678). 4. Select the route table. 5. Under Name, choose the edit icon (the pencil), enter a name (for example, appstream-public-routetable), then click the check mark to save. 6. On the Routes tab, confirm one destination and target for local traffic and another destination and target that sends all other traffic to the internet gateway (example, igw-0518a307898725db2). Internal Only - General 7. In the left pane, choose Subnets. 8. Select the first private subnet that you created (example, AppStream Private subnet 1) 9. On the Route Table tab, click the ID of the route table (example, rtb-12345678). 10. Select the route table. Under Name, choose the edit icon (the pencil), enter a name (for example, appstream-private-routetable1), then click the check mark to save. 11. On the Routes tab, confirm one destination and target for local traffic and another destination and target that sends all other traffic to the NAT gateway (example, nat-06ea352539b2fddfc). 12. In the left pane, choose Subnets. 13. Select the second private subnet that you created (example, AppStream Private subnet 2) 14. On the Route Table tab, click the ID of the route table (example, rtb-12345678). 15. Select the route table. Under Name, choose the edit icon (the pencil), enter a name (for example, appstream-private-routetable2), then click the check mark to save. 16. On the Routes tab, confirm one destination and target for local traffic and another destination and target that sends all other traffic to the NAT gateway (example, nat-06ea352539b2fddfc).
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that AWS AppStream fleet VPC assignments are fully exposed via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The required information (VPC configuration for each AppStream fleet) can be programmatically retrieved and evaluated for compliance. There is no need for manual inspection, as all necessary properties are accessible and verifiable through standard AWS SDK calls.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: AppStream Fleets - API/CLI Method: boto3 client('appstream').describe_fleets() or aws appstream describe-fleets - Properties to Check: For each fleet, examine the VpcConfig.VpcId property. Ensure each fleet is assigned to a dedicated (non-shared) VPC, and that no fleet is running without a VPC or in a default/shared VPC. - Pass Criteria: Every AppStream fleet has a VpcConfig with a unique VpcId that is not used by other non-AppStream resources and is not the default/shared VPC. - Fail Criteria: Any AppStream fleet is missing a VpcConfig, is assigned to a default/shared VPC, or shares a VPC with unrelated resources.
default_value: By default, there is no VPC tied to the Appstream 2.0 service.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure a VPC Endpoint is set for AppStream
description: When you select Using a VPC endpoint, this allows users to only stream from this AppStream 2.0 stack when they have network access to the VPC.
rationale: Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) endpoints allow your users to stream from AppStream 2.0 through your VPC. You can create a VPC endpoint in the VPC of your choosing, then use the endpoint with AppStream 2.0 VPC to maintain the streaming traffic within the VPC.
audit: Perform the steps to review the interface endpoint set for AppStream 2.0 From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane, click Stacks, click the link for the stack you wish to view. 3. Scroll to the VPC Endpoints section. 4. Confirm the Streaming Endpoint listed is the endpoint through which to stream traffic. If there is no Streaming endpoint pointing to a specific VPC Endpoint and it is labeled as Internet refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to create an interface endpoint From the Console: 1. Log in to the VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/ 2. In the left pane, click Endpoints, Create Endpoint. 3. Click Create Endpoint. Internal Only - General - For Service category, ensure that AWS services is selected. - For Service Name, choose com.amazonaws.<AWS Region>.appstream.streaming. - For VPC, choose a VPC in which to create the interface endpoint. - For Subnets, choose the subnets (Availability Zones) in which to create the endpoint network interfaces. - Ensure that the Enable Private DNS Name check box is selected. - For Security group, select the security group for AppStream 4. Click Create endpoint. To update a stack to use a new interface endpoint 1. Log in to AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane, click Stacks, and click the link of the stack name wish to edit. 3. Scroll to the VPC Endpoints, and then choose Edit. 4. In the Edit VPC Endpoint dialog box, for Streaming Endpoint, choose the endpoint you just created. 5. Click Save Changes. Traffic for new streaming sessions will be routed through this endpoint. However, traffic for current streaming sessions continues to be routed through the previously specified endpoint.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the presence and configuration of a VPC Endpoint for AppStream can be fully verified using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The required properties—such as the existence, state, and association of the VPC Endpoint with the AppStream service—are all exposed via the EC2 API (`describe_vpc_endpoints`). No manual inspection is needed, as all relevant data is programmatically accessible and auditable.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: VPC Endpoints for AppStream (EC2 VPC Endpoints) - API/CLI Method: - boto3: `ec2.describe_vpc_endpoints()` - AWS CLI: `aws ec2 describe-vpc-endpoints --filters "Name=service-name,Values=com.amazonaws.<region>.appstream2"` - Properties to Check: - `ServiceName` equals `com.amazonaws.<region>.appstream2` - `State` is `available` - `VpcId` matches the intended VPC for AppStream - (Optional but recommended) `SubnetIds` and `RouteTableIds` are correctly associated as per AppStream deployment requirements - Pass Criteria: - At least one VPC Endpoint exists for `com.amazonaws.<region>.appstream2` in the intended VPC, is in the `available` state, and is associated with the correct subnets/route tables. - Fail Criteria: - No VPC Endpoint exists for AppStream, or it is not in the `available` state, or it is not associated with the correct VPC/subnets.
default_value: By default, VPC endpoints must be manually configured.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure maximum session duration is no longer than 10 hours
description: When creating a fleet for AppStream 2.0 configure the Maximum session duration in minutes to be no greater than 600.
rationale: Having a session duration lasting longer than 10 hours should not be necessary and if running for any malicious reasons provides a greater time for usage than should be allowed.
audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. On the Fleet configuration section confirm that Maximum session duration is set to 600 minutes or less. If Maximum session duration is set to anything greater than 600 minutes refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to edit the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to edit. 4. Click Actions, Stop 5. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and click Edit 6. Change the Maximum session duration is set to 600 minutes or less and click Save Changes 7. Click Actions, Start Internal Only - General
justification: Both AI opinions agree that this control can be fully automated using AWS APIs, specifically by retrieving all IAM roles and checking their MaxSessionDuration attribute. The required property is directly accessible via boto3 (`get_role`) and AWS CLI (`aws iam get-role`). No manual inspection is necessary, and the check is straightforward and reliable using programmatic methods.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: IAM Roles - API/CLI Method: - boto3: `iam.list_roles()`, `iam.get_role(RoleName=...)` - AWS CLI: `aws iam list-roles`, `aws iam get-role --role-name <ROLE_NAME>` - Properties to Check: `MaxSessionDuration` attribute of each IAM role - Pass Criteria: Every IAM role has `MaxSessionDuration` set to 36,000 seconds (10 hours) or less - Fail Criteria: Any IAM role has `MaxSessionDuration` set to more than 36,000 seconds
default_value: By default, AWS Appstream 2.0 maximum session time is 960 minutes.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure session disconnect timeout is set to 5 minutes or less
description: Disconnect timeout in minutes, is the amount of of time that a streaming session remains active after users disconnect.
rationale: If users try to reconnect to the streaming session after a disconnection or network interruption within the 5 minutes, they are connected to their previous session. Otherwise, they are connected to a new session with a new streaming instance and that instance isn't sitting out there not being used.
audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. On the Fleet configuration section confirm that Disconnect timeout is set to 5 minutes minutes or less. If Disconnect timeout is set to anything greater than 5 minutes refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to update the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to edit. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and click Edit 5. Change the Disconnect timeout to 5 minutes or less. Internal Only - General
justification: Despite the first opinion suggesting a manual approach, AWS services such as AppStream 2.0 and WorkSpaces expose session disconnect timeout settings via their APIs (e.g., DescribeStacks for AppStream 2.0). These APIs allow programmatic retrieval and verification of session timeout configurations, making automation feasible and reliable. The second opinion correctly identifies this capability, and AWS documentation confirms these properties are accessible via boto3 and AWS CLI.
automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: AppStream 2.0 Stacks - API/CLI Method: boto3 appstream.describe_stacks() or aws appstream describe-stacks - Properties to Check: Each stack's 'DisconnectTimeoutInSeconds' property - Pass Criteria: 'DisconnectTimeoutInSeconds' is set to 300 seconds (5 minutes) or less for all stacks - Fail Criteria: Any stack has 'DisconnectTimeoutInSeconds' set to more than 300 seconds, or the property is unset
default_value: By default, AWS Appstream 2.0 session disconnect time is set to 15 minutes.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure session Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less
description: Idle disconnect timeout in minutes is the amount of time that users can be inactive before they are disconnected from their streaming session and the Disconnect timeout in minutes time begins.
rationale: Users are considered idle when they stop providing keyboard or mouse input during their streaming session. File uploads and downloads, audio in, audio out, and pixels changing do not qualify as user activity. Once disconnected from their streaming session the Disconnect timeout begins.
audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and confirm that Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less. If Idle disconnect timeout is set to anything greater than 10 minutes refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and click Edit 5. Change the Idle disconnect timeout to 5 minutes or less and click Save changes Internal Only - General
justification: While the first AI opinion suggests automation via boto3, the second opinion correctly points out that the session idle disconnect timeout for AWS WorkSpaces is not directly accessible through any current AWS API, CLI, or SDK method. This setting is managed via the AWS Console under WorkSpaces client properties and is not exposed programmatically for audit. Therefore, verification must be performed manually through the AWS Console.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Amazon WorkSpaces service. 2. In the left navigation pane, select "Directories." 3. For each directory, click on the directory name to open its details. 4. Under the "Access Control Options" or "User Access" section, locate the "Client Properties" or "Session Settings." 5. Verify that the "Idle Disconnect Timeout" is set to 10 minutes (600 seconds) or less. 6. Repeat this process for all directories and WorkSpaces in your environment. 7. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
default_value: By default, AWS Appstream 2.0 idle disconnect time is15 minutes

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure internet access is granted and managed through your VPC
description: Default Internet Access from your fleet streaming instances should remain unchecked.
rationale: When Default Internet Access is enabled, AppStream 2.0 uses the internet gateway in the VPC public subnet to connect to the public internet. The streaming instances are then assigned public IP addresses that are directly accessible from the internet. Internet Access from fleet streaming instances should be controlled using a NAT gateway in the VPC. When Default Internet Access is not enabled, streaming instances are assigned a private IP address that are not directly accessible from the internet.
audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. On the Network details section confirm that Default Internet Access is set to Disabled. If Default internet access is not set to disabled refer to the remediation below.
remediation: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to edit. 4. Click Actions, Stop 5. Scroll to the Network details section and click Edit 6. Deselect Default Internet Access 7. Click Save changes Internal Only - General
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that all relevant VPC internet access configurations—such as route tables, internet gateways, and their associations—are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). There is no requirement for manual inspection or console-only data; all necessary properties are programmatically retrievable and auditable. This makes the control ideally suited for automation.
automation_summary: - **AWS Resource/Service:** VPCs, Route Tables, Internet Gateways, Network ACLs, Security Groups - **API/CLI Method:** - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_vpcs()` - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_route_tables()` - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_internet_gateways()` - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_network_acls()` - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_security_groups()` - **Properties to Check:** - Route tables with routes targeting an Internet Gateway (IGW) - Associations between route tables and subnets - Existence and attachment of IGWs to VPCs - Network ACLs and Security Groups for rules allowing unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0) access - **Pass Criteria:** - All internet access is routed through explicitly managed IGWs associated with the VPC. - No subnets have unintended direct internet access (e.g., via misconfigured route tables). - Network ACLs and Security Groups do not allow unrestricted inbound/outbound internet access unless explicitly required and documented. - **Fail Criteria:** - Any subnet has a route to an IGW not managed through the VPC. - IGWs are attached to VPCs without proper route table configuration. - Network ACLs or Security Groups allow unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0) access without justification.
default_value: By default, internet access is not enabled; the box is "unchecked" in the management console.

source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
